paper_title: Species Distribution and Environmental Influence Analysis

methods.method_name: Principal Component Analysis (PCA) for Environmental Influence
methods.description: This method uses Principal Component Analysis (PCA) to examine the variance and correlation across 19 environmental measurements to understand the distribution of B. morio and B. pauloensis species.

steps.description: Collect environmental measurements from WorldClim for the sampled range of B. morio and B. pauloensis.
Perform PCA to examine variance and correlation across the environmental measurements.
Plot environmental conditions at a single point per grid cell per mitochondrial lineage for each species.

methods.description: This method verifies the influence of altitude on the distribution of B. morio and B. pauloensis by extracting altitude values for sampling localities.

steps.description: Extract altitude values for sampling localities using GTOPO 30 digital elevation model.

methods.description: This method involves creating maps using R to visualize the distribution of species and environmental data.

steps.description: Create maps using R with Raster and Maptools packages.
steps.searchable_summary: generate maps in R using Raster and Maptools

methods.description: This method describes the collection of bee specimens and the extraction of DNA using the Chelex® 100 method.

steps.description: Collect 183 individuals of B. morio and 221 B. pauloensis during field trips and from museum collections.
steps.searchable_summary: collect bee specimens from field and museums

steps.description: Perform DNA extraction using the Chelex® 100 method.
